definition: Compare point-cloud or point-set samples using Chamfer distance, Earth Mover's Distance, sliced Wasserstein distance, or a domain-specific geometric distance, then transform distance to similarity. This audits whether performance degrades for shapes or structures geometrically far from the training set.
example_usage: python point_cloud_chamfer_similarity.py --train train_clouds.csv --eval eval_clouds.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --points-col points --scale 1.0

citations.title: Point-set Distances for Learning Representations of 3D Point Clouds

evidence.summary: The paper studies point-cloud distances including Chamfer, Earth Mover's, and sliced Wasserstein distances.
It defines Chamfer discrepancy and EMD for point clouds and motivates more discriminative Wasserstein variants.
Sliced Wasserstein is evaluated across reconstruction, transfer, registration, and generation tasks.

limitations: Chamfer distance can be insensitive to distributional differences and outliers.
EMD and Wasserstein variants can be computationally expensive.
Point alignment, scale normalization, and coordinate frames can dominate similarity.
quality_gates: Report point preprocessing, alignment, scaling, distance metric, and distance-to-similarity transform.
Use EMD or sliced Wasserstein when point density and global geometry matter.
Check sensitivity to subsampling and registration errors.
